title: Reject allowlisted backup paths that escape through symlinks
prompt: The local backup utility is complete, but its source authorization check can be bypassed with a symlink beneath an allowlisted directory. Repair the path authorization boundary so an explicitly requested symlink and every symlink found below a requested directory are resolved against the configured allowlist before any snapshot is published. A symlink whose resolved target is outside every allowlisted root (or cannot be resolved) must make the backup fail without changing `latest` or leaving a staging snapshot. Symlinks resolving inside an allowlisted root must remain supported. Preserve the existing CLI, manifest/checksum format, incremental hard-link behavior, atomic publication, retention, lock handling, partial-failure cleanup, and exact restore instruction output. Do not modify tests. Run `bash tests/smoke.sh` while developing; the protected acceptance command is `bash tests/acceptance.sh`.
